---
title: Transcription factors identified using AME vs pySCENIC
date: 2023-04-20
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To determine which TFs were identified through both AME and pySCENIC in our homologous and non-homologous UBC populations.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<human Aldinger software directory>/ame_pyscenic_common_genes.R`, `<utilities>/pyscenic_regulons.R`, and `<utilities>/plot_venn_diagrams.R`.

pySCENIC analysis from 2023-04-02 identified 42 regulons in total across all the cell types. The regulons were ranked by their RSS for each cell type, and only the top 50% (21 regulators aka TFs) from each cell type were compared to the AME TFs (code in `pyscenic_regulons.R`).

The non-homologous UBCs consisted of two populations; as such, the union of the TFs from the two populations was taken (i.e., 19-NonHomol UBC TFs ∪ 20-NonHomol UBC TFs).

AME results were taken from 2023-04-19.

### Output

Common TFs in the homologous UBCs are found in the `homol_ubc/` subdirectory.

Common TFs in the non-homologous UBCs are found in the `nonhomol_ubc/` subdirectory.

Each subdirectory contains, for its specific cell type, the list of enriched TF-binding motifs from AME (`ame_tfs.txt`) and the list of top-scoring (by RSS) pySCENIC regulators (`pyscenic_tfs.txt`).


## Results

![TFs identified by both AME and pySCENIC in homologous UBCs.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230420/homol_ubc/ame_pyscenic_genes_venn.png){#fig-homol-tfs}

![TFs identified by both AME and pySCENIC in non-homologous UBCs.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230420/nonhomol_ubc/ame_pyscenic_genes_venn.png){#fig-nonhomol-tfs}
